tr: Old pepper tree,
Today I come to visit you again,
They say you know
How to heal poets’ troubles.
I was born with my heart exposed,

es: desde niño quiere más que latir.
tr: Since childhood it has wanted more than to beat.

es: No quiere que yo le cuente del mundo,
tr: It doesn’t want me to tell it about the world,

es: por sí mismo él lo quiere sentir.
tr: It wants to feel it for itself.
Old pepper tree,
My refuge and my place.
Naked heart,
It feels everything:
Lantern in storms,

es: Pero se quiebra con un mirar.
tr: But it breaks with a glance.
It loves fast, without measure.
It finds beauty in the simple.

es: Pero en su sangre carga con penas ajenas,
tr: But in its blood it carries others’ sorrows,
And melts away.
If it insists on staying outside,
Let it beat in your shade.
Old pepper tree,

es: Fuiste el juego en mi infancia.
tr: You were the game of my childhood.
I cover myself with your leaves
To be born again.
